title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants `validate()` to guard against an empty `RotationSchedule` string by adding an explicit empty-string check before calling `cron.ParseStandard`, so that callers outside the normal Kubernetes admission pipeline (e.g., tests, CLI tools) get a clear "required" error instead of a confusing cron parse failure.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Addressed: Replaced the unconditional cron.ParseStandard(k.Spec.Fernet.RotationSchedule) call with an if/else-if guard — empty string now produces a field.Required error ('rotationSchedule must be set; default is "0 0 * * 0"'), and non-empty strings are validated via cron.ParseStandard as before. Added the reviewer's recommended NOTE comment documenting the CRD marker ordering dependency. Added TestValidate_EmptyRotationScheduleReturnsRequiredError test to cover the new empty-string branch.

resolution_summary: The review feedback was addressed exactly as suggested. In keystone_webhook.go, the unconditional cron.ParseStandard call was replaced with an empty-string guard that returns a field.Required error with the message 'rotationSchedule must be set; default is "0 0 * * 0"', falling through to the cron parse check only when the value is non-empty. The recommended comment documenting the CRD marker ordering dependency was added verbatim. A new unit test TestValidate_EmptyRotationScheduleReturnsRequiredError was added in keystone_webhook_test.go to exercise the empty-string path. No files outside those directly mentioned in the review were changed.
